title: Croke
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Species
|image=[[File:RokurGepta.png]]
|name=Croke
|designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>
|class=Either [[Arthropod/Legends|arthropods]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> or [[Mollusk/Legends|mollusks]]<ref name="TUR">''[[The Unknown Regions]]''</ref>
|subspecies=
|races=
|height=
|length=Small enough to fit in a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Limb/Legends|hand]];<ref name="JATM">''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]''</ref> no bigger than a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee's]] fist<ref name="TDSS">''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]''</ref>
|wingspan=
|mass=
|skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Black]],<ref name="CSWE I 153">{{CSWECite|I|153|Croke}}</ref> gray<ref name="TDSS" />
|haircolor=
|feathers=
|eyecolor=
|distinctions=Extended lifespan;<ref name="SoT">''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]''</ref> natural [[Mind trick/Legends|illusion]] ability;<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> [[Oil/Legends|oily]] skin;<ref name="CSWE I 153" /> many legs;<ref name="TDSS" /> [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]] resistance<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>
|lifespan=Over 20,000 [[Standard year|years]]<ref name="SoT" />
|origin=[[Crakull]]<ref name="TNEGtC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref>
|habitat=
|diet=
|language=[[Croke (language)|Croke]]<ref name="TUR" />
}}
{{Quote|Crokes are nearly impervious to hard vacuum, and this particular Croke was still alive. He was also responsible for more murders than you can count, friend, so no, I did not hesitate to squeeze that vicious thing to death, even though I knew it would be quite a chore to get the mess off my gloves.|Lando Calrissian, ''How to Succeed in Everything''|<ref name="JvS" />}}
The '''Croke''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], multi-[[Limb/Legends|legged]] beings from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Crakull]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] who were extremely long lived; one individual enjoyed a lifespan of more than 20,000 [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. In their natural form, the species resembled bristly-legged [[Snail/Legends|snails]] that were diminutive enough to be held in a [[Human/Legends|Human]] hand. However, Croke were naturally skilled in [[Mind trick/Legends|illusion]] casting, a [[Force power/Legends|Force ability]] that allowed them to appear in larger guises.

The Croke colonized a larger area of the Unknown Regions known as the [[Croke Reach]] and had a reputation in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for being unpleasant and vicious. At one point, the Croke fought a long and debilitating [[Croke civil war|civil war]]. The destruction worsened greatly when one of the factions introduced a voracious species known as the [[Lugubraa]]—commonly employed as [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] in the Unknown Regions—as a weapon into the Reach's worlds. Many Croke-settled planets, such as [[Explume Minor]], were destroyed by the Lugubraa, whose unfettered [[Reproduction/Legends|reproductive]] rate led them to feed voraciously. To wipe out the invasive species, the Croke purchased a genetically modified strain of Lugubraa, known as [[blind berserker]]s, from the [[Cloning/Legends|cloners]] of [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. [[Leech Legion|These clones]], however, proved to be just as problematic as the original Lugubraa, and caused further devastation to the Croke Reach. The Croke species was nearly wiped out.

One Croke, [[Rokur Gepta]], left his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] and began a reign of terror in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Gepta massacred a group of mystics known as the [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerers of Tund]] and later allied himself with the ruler of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], who gave Gepta control of an area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] known as the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]]. Gepta came into conflict with the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], whom the Croke swindled into doing his bidding. The young smuggler eventually turned on Gepta and cost him control of a powerful artifact known as the [[Mindharp of Sharu/Legends|Mindharp]]. After Calrissian became trapped by an Imperial [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]], Gepta [[Battle of ThonBoka|attempted]] to [[Death/Legends|kill]] him and all members of a species known as the [[Oswaft]] with a [[Rokur Gepta's biological weapon|biological weapon]]. Gepta's plans were foiled on both accounts, and Calrissian personally shot the Croke and crushed him to death.

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|I pawed through the suit until I found something down in the left leg. It was a small bundle of ugly, slimy tissue, resembling a half-cooked slug with a dozen skinny, hairy black legs.|Lando Calrissian, ''How to Succeed in Everything''|<ref name="JvS" />}}
The Croke were tiny compared to most other [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]];<ref name="TNEC" /> an individual could fit into a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Limb/Legends|hand]],<ref name="JATM" /> and no member of the species grew larger than a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee's]] fist.<ref name="TDSS" /> The beings were either [[Arthropod/Legends|arthropods]]<ref name="TEA" /> or [[Mollusk/Legends|mollusks]],<ref name="TUR" /> and their appearance was grotesque and unattractive by Human standards.<ref name="SoT" /> The Croke body resembled that of a [[Snail/Legends|snail]]<ref name="AGttSWU2">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> or [[Slug (creature)/Legends|slug]],<ref name="JvS" /> with [[Oil/Legends|oily]] skin that ranged from [[Color/Legends|black]]<ref name="CSWE I 153" /> to gray. A Croke crept about on spindly, hairy, black legs;<ref name="TDSS" /><ref name="CSWE I 334">{{CSWECite|I|334|Gepta, Rokur}}</ref> a single individual might have sported as few as a dozen<ref name="JvS" /> or as many as several thousand such appendages.<ref name="TDSS" /> As tiny creatures, Croke were vulnerable to being squashed by larger beings, and they exuded a greasy slime when crushed. Croke were able to survive in a [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]] for extended periods.<ref name="SoT" /> At least one member of the species lived an extremely long lifespan; he died from injury after reaching more than 20,000 [[Standard year/Legends|standard years]] of age.<ref name="SoT" />

Croke had a natural ability to disguise themselves behind [[Mind trick/Legends|illusions]]. The mechanics of this [[Force power/Legends|power]] were ill understood by members of other species—one point of contention was whether such illusions merely affected the minds of living beings or were also able to fool electronic [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]].<ref name="TNEGtC" /> In reality, the power was based on manipulation of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="TDSS" /> With this natural ability, the Croke were masters of disguise, able to hide their true form indefinitely<ref name="JvS" /> and able to trick even the most keen minds and perceptive targets. Indeed, no one viewing a Croke in illusory form was able to penetrate the disguise unless the Croke became [[Stun/Legends|stunned]] or [[Sleep/Legends|lost consciousness]].<ref name="TDSS" /> Using such natural camouflage,<ref name="JATM" /> a Croke could take on the shape or appearance of anything up to three [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height.<ref name="CSWE I 334" /> A Croke did not need to appear identically to all who saw the illusion maker; the Croke might spin separate illusions for different viewers simultaneously.<ref name="JATM" /> For instance, the Croke [[Rokur Gepta]] presented himself as a dwarf to some who encountered him, and as a three-meter giant to others.<ref name="CSWE I 334" /> Such illusions fooled all of their target's senses, making such disguises seem completely real even to touch. Nevertheless, such disguises did not grant the Croke additional strength, speed, or other abilities.<ref name="JATM" /> Camouflaged Croke had to be physically present somewhere within an illusory form; an individual might appear to be [[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoid]] and hide in the form's foot, for instance. Only damage sustained in the region where the Croke's physical body was located affected the actual Croke.<ref name="TNEGtC" /> Despite such disguises' artifice, Croke were able to wield weapons and objects as if their illusory form were real.<ref name="TDSS" /> At least some members of the species also were able to disguise smaller objects, such as Croke young.<ref name="TUR" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|I recognized it as a Croke, an intelligent but remarkably nasty species, all masters of camouflage and illusion.|Lando Calrissian, ''How to Succeed in Everything''|<ref name="JvS" />}}
The Croke [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Crakull]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]].<ref name="TNEGtC" /> The species also occupied a group of Unknown Regions worlds known as the [[Croke Reach]]. [[Explume Minor]] was one such planet. These Croke settlements could communicate with one another via transmissions. Croke settlements protected their young, who were referred to as "[[Youngling/Legends|nestlings]]," and certain individuals in Croke society were known as [[Shell Conjurer|shell conjurers]]. The species was in contact with groups who lived beyond the Croke Reach, such as the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoans]].<ref name="TUR" />

Groups who knew of the Croke considered them one of the many dangers of the Unknown Regions.<ref name="TEA" /> The diminutive beings were known to have vicious personalities<ref name="CSWE I 153" /> characterized by mean-spiritedness<ref name="JvS" /> and blatant pursuit of personal ends.<ref name="TDSS" /> Croke compensated for their tiny size with their natural mastery of the Force and the casting of illusions over their true form.<ref name="JATM" /> The species spoke a language also known as [[Croke (language)|Croke]],<ref name="TUR" /> although individuals were capable of learning to read and write languages such as [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], [[Sith (language)|Sith]], and [[Tundan]] as well.<ref name="TDSS" /> Some Croke swore hexes when vexed.<ref name="TUR" />

==History==
{{Quote|Explume is lost to the Lugubraa, six hexes be upon them. Cloak and evacuate the nestlings while I—''awwwwkk!''|Shell Conjurer Firtha|<ref name="TUR" />}}
[[File:Lugubraa-TUR.png|thumb|left|Croke factions employed Lugubraa mercenaries during a civil war and nearly wiped themselves out.]]
The Croke expanded from their homeworld, Crakull, to occupy multiple worlds within an area that came to be known as the Croke Reach.<ref name="TUR" /> During this expansion, the species came into contact with non-Croke, including the [[Cloning/Legends|cloners]] of [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]].<ref name="TUR" /> For several decades, the Croke resisted incursions into their territory by the sentient entity known as [[Mnggal-Mnggal]].<ref name="TUR" />

Approximately 300 years before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Croke were engaged in a [[Croke civil war|civil war]] of their own. Some factions employed biological weapons in the form of a species known as the [[Lugubraa]], hulking creatures whose natural fecundity and unvarnished appetites led them to overwhelm entire ecosystems. The Croke who employed these weapons unleashed small populations of Lugubraa on planets controlled by their rivals. The tactic proved too successful, and the diminutive species found itself faced by hordes of Lugubraa long into the period after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Facing the extinction of their very species, the Croke eventually solicited the aid of Kaminoan cloning experts to engineer a genetic variant of the Lugubraa that might wipe out the hordes menacing Croke worlds. The Kaminoans provided a [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] type known as the "[[blind berserker]]." Nevertheless, this new force, dubbed the "[[Leech Legion]]," proved just as destructive to Croke settlements.<ref name="TUR" /><ref name="TEA" /> At some point during these campaigns, Lugubraa attacked the planet Explume Minor. [[Shell Conjurer]] [[Firtha]] sent a transmission to order the Croke to cloak and evacuate their nestlings before his transmission cut out.<ref name="TUR" />

==Crokes in the galaxy==
{{Quote|To tell the truth, even though the last Sorcerer of Tund is long dead, I'm not very keen on talking about him. Mind you, my disinclination isn't out of any respect. It's because he was the kind of scary that doesn't die easily, and not talking about him is the way I prefer to keep him dead.|Lando Calrissian, ''How to Succeed in Everything''|<ref name="JvS" />}}
[[File:LandoGepta.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Lando Calrissian faced the Croke Rokur Gepta, who was disguised as a tall humanoid in a turban.]]
Some Crokes left the bounds of the Croke Reach and made their way into the [[The galaxy/Legends|known galaxy]]. Such members of the species typically used their natural abilities of camouflage and disguise to their advantage<ref name="AGttSWU2" /> so as to conceal their true form from outsiders and pass unnoticed among them.<ref name="TDSS" /> This was not true for all expatriate Crokes however: [[Asenec]] was a Croke [[Author/Legends|writer]] and [[Poet/Legends|poet]] active from [[593 BBY]] to [[244 BBY/Legends|244 BBY]], and the identity of his species was publicly known. Asenec's poetry was regarded even centuries later as sickening, repulsive, and deliberately offensive, with his themes celebrating atrocities and tyranny. Asenec's works were rumored to be a favorite of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], and he was considered to be the most banned writer in the civilized galaxy.<ref name="Author's Cut">{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-3-xim-the-despot|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 3 - "Xim the Despot"}}</ref>

One of the more notorious members of his species to leave the Unknown Regions was Rokur Gepta, who regularly presented himself as a 1.8-meter-tall humanoid dressed in gray robes and a turban, an outfit that left only his [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] exposed.<ref name="TNEGtC" /> Gepta infiltrated a group of mystics known as the [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerers of Tund]] in [[5 BBY/Legends|5 BBY]],<ref name="TDSS" /> learned their secrets, and then wiped them out.<ref name="UPoEI">{{InsiderCite|56|Unseen Planets of Episode I}}</ref> Gepta came to the attention of Emperor Palpatine, who allowed the sorcerer free reign in the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] region and special power over the local representatives of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in exchange for arcane knowledge that Gepta had learned from the sorcerers.<ref name="TNEGtC" />

Nevertheless, Gepta was unsatisfied and desired to rule the whole [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. To that end, he sought a powerful artifact called the [[Mindharp of Sharu/Legends|Mindharp]]. In his quest, Gepta enlisted the aid of a Human [[Gambling/Legends|gambler]] named [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]].<ref name="JvS" /> When Gepta was betrayed by his [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]], [[Duttes Mer]],<ref name="MoS">''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]''</ref> the Croke blamed Calrissian for this failure and attempted to take out his vengeance on the gambler. After [[Death/Legends|killing]] a wealthy man named [[Bohhuah Mutdah/Legends|Bohhuah Mutdah]], Gepta impersonated him as he sent Calrissian on a [[Spice/Legends|drug]]-running mission to the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] [[Oseon 5792]]. There, the sorcerer [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] his nemesis, but the Human escaped. Rokur Gepta next pursued his target to the [[ThonBoka]] [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]].<ref name="JvS" /> There, the sorcerer threatened to destroy a species known as the [[Oswaft]] with a [[Rokur Gepta's biological weapon|biological weapon]] unless Calrissian agreed to a face-to-face duel.<ref name="SoT" /> Calrissian reciprocated, and Gepta [[Battle of ThonBoka|confronted]] Calrissian in the vacuum of [[Realspace/Legends|space]], both combatants clad in [[Environment suit/Legends|flight suits]]. Calrissian shot the Croke's illusory form in the ankle, where Gepta's actual body was located, and then crushed the Croke in his hand.<ref name="JvS" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The pull-quote for this entry brings in the Croke, a species of shelled spiders of which Rokur Gepta is a member. The Croke also live in the Unknown Regions and I felt kind of bad about not being able to cover them in detail, so they got at least a shout-out here…|Daniel Wallace|<ref name="DanBlog">{{Blog|danwallace|168|Endnotes for Star Wars: The Unknown Regions}}</ref>}}
Author [[L. Neil Smith]] created the Croke for his [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''Lando Calrissian'' trilogy]] of novels: ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'', ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'', and ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]'', all published in [[1983]]. Gepta maintains an illusory form through most of Neil's trilogy; his true form is only revealed during the climax of the third book during his final confrontation with Calrissian. Later sources, such as ''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' ([[2001]]) and ''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' ([[2010]]), have expanded on the Croke and their history. For example, ''The Dark Side Sourcebook'', written by [[Bill Slavicsek]] and [[J.D. Wiker|J. D. Wiker]] for the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' from [[Wizards of the Coast]], includes game statistics for the species. These rules refer to the species' natural illusion-making power as "Croke illusory presence." The book also grants Croke characters bonuses to all uses of disguise or illusion.<ref name="TDSS" /> Meanwhile, ''The Unknown Regions'' establishes the Croke's relations with the Lugubraa in a section written by [[Daniel Wallace]]. In an entry for his blog, Wallace lamented that he did not have the opportunity to expand on the Croke further.<ref name="DanBlog" />

Another roleplaying book, [[2009]]'s ''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]'', written by [[Eric Cagle]], [[Patrick Stutzman]], [[Rodney Thompson]], and [[Robert Wieland]], claims that all members of the Sorcerers of Tund were Croke,<ref name="JATM" /> a statement that conflicts with other sources. For instance, a web enhancement written by [[Abel G. Peña]] for the [[2008]] book ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' introduces a Sorcerer of Tund named [[Karnak Tetsu]] who is a [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith Offshoot]].<ref name="WizardsCite-KOTOR Campaign Guide Web Enhancement 5: Karnak Tetsu, Sorcerer of Tund">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/KOTORweben5|text=KOTOR Campaign Guide Web Enhancement 5: Karnak Tetsu, Sorcerer of Tund}}</ref> Similarly, ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' ([[2009]]) implies that when Rokur Gepta infiltrated the Sorcerers of Tund, they did not know he was a Croke.<ref name="TEA" /> Finally, [[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']] ([[2010]]) claims the Sorcerers descended from pure-blooded members of the Sith species.<ref name="TJP">[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']]</ref> This article follows these later sources and assumes that the ''Jedi Academy Training Manual'' is in error.
